justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with cell-based architecture where no raw PII crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed structurally (region-primary ownership, no cross-region replication). Global dashboard reads exclusively from Global Metrics Aggregator which consumes only sanitized/anonymized metrics via the Metrics Bridge ACL — no cross-region raw data access. Courier assignment regional isolation enforced via immutable region binding on courier records. Trade-offs explicitly cover 3-region operational overhead (infrastructure cost, configuration drift, code duplication). Degraded EU service designed with Resilience Layer providing cached read paths during outage while new orders queue locally.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough regional isolation model with cell-based architecture — each region has its own complete stack including gateway, services, database, and event bus
Metrics Bridge as explicit Anti-Corruption Layer with PII allowlist approach is a strong compliance design — better than the minimum requirement
Defense-in-depth for routing: GeoDNS + gateway domicile validation + service-level checks prevents accidental cross-region data access
Risks section is comprehensive — covers PII leakage via schema evolution, GeoDNS misrouting, configuration drift, and aggregator failure
Phased implementation guidance (refactor EU first, then US, then APAC) is practical and reduces rollout risk
Degraded service design includes specific mechanisms: read-through cache for order status, staleness indicators, local queuing for new orders
Trade-offs are well-structured with alternatives_considered showing rejected approaches and rationale

Could have been more explicit about CCPA-specific requirements (e.g., right to deletion, opt-out of sale) beyond general data residency — GDPR and CCPA are mentioned but treated somewhat interchangeably

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC with strict data residency (GDPR/CCPA), region-bound courier assignment, a global operations dashboard, graceful degradation, and sub-100ms regional latency. The architecture uses a region-primary ownership model: each region owns its customer and courier data authoritatively, enforces residency at the infrastructure boundary, and publishes anonymized/aggregated operational metrics to a global
